drupalNodeEvent.title: Daoism, Ecology and Sustainable Investing

field_organizers.processed: Hong Kong Institute for the Humanities and Social Sciences, The University of Hong Kong

drupalNodeEvent.field_event_venue: Lecture Hall, G/F, May Hall, The University of Hong Kong

field_description.processed:
KEYNOTE SPEAKER
Prof James Miller (Author of China’s Green Religion: Daoism and the Quest for a Sustainable Future) (Duke Kunshan University)
主題發言人：苗建時教授 (Prof James Miller)（杜克崑山大學）

ABOUT THE SPEAKER
His research focuses on the intersection of religion and ecology in China. He has published six books including “China’s Green Religion: Daoism and the Quest for a Sustainable Future” (Columbia, 2017). He is noted worldwide as an expert in Daoism, China’s indigenous religion. His teaching interests at Duke Kunshan include ethics and leadership, global China studies, environmental science, U.S. studies, religious studies and philosophy.

Miller has a B.A. (honors) in Chinese studies from Durham University, a B.A. (honors) and M.A. in theological and religious studies from Cambridge University, and a Ph.D. in religious and theological studies from Boston University. Before joining Duke Kunshan, he was director of the School of Religion at Queen’s University, Canada.

OPENING REMARK
Venerable Daoist Master ZHANG Mingxin (Vice President of Chinese Daoist Association; President of Mount Qingcheng Daoist Association)
開幕致詞： 張明心 道長（中國道教協會副會長，青城山道教協會會長）

DISCUSSANTS
Martin PALMER (Founding Director of the Green Climate Fund Faith Art Media Alliance)
Anthony CHEUNG (Vice Chairman and Convenor of Green Finance, Friends of the Earth (HK)
LI Haibo (Chairman of Chengdu Daoist Entrepreneurship and Technology Research Institute)

評述人
彭馬田 (Martin PALMER) (聯合國全球氣候變化框架綠色氣候基金信仰藝術傳播聯盟創始總監)
張震宇 （香港地球之友副主席）
李海波（成都市道商科技研究院院長）

HOST
Prof. David A. Palmer (Professor, Sociology & HKIHSS, HKU)
主持人：宗樹人教授（香港大學社會學及香港人文社會研究所）
